mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Excess inflation (CPIAUCSL > 2.5% YoY) triggers central bank tightening, increasing discount rates and reducing equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite inflationary signal, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDP growth (GDPC1) combined with declining unemployment (UNRATE) signals robust economic demand, driving corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Interaction term between GDPC1 and UNRATE shows Rank IC < 0.03, or 20d returns are negative in > 40% of instances where both proxies indicate strong demand.

mechanism_narrative: During high unemployment regimes (UNRATE > 7%), rising payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals labor market recovery, boosting consumer spending and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Interaction term between PAYEMS and UNRATE shows Rank IC < 0.03, or 20d returns are negative in > 40% of instances where unemployment remains above 7%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) indicates strong supply-side capacity utilization, reducing input costs and improving corporate margins, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO shows no correlation with SPX returns (Rank IC < 0.03) over any 3-year rolling window, or 20d returns are negative in > 40% of instances with rising INDPRO.

mechanism_narrative: A surge in housing starts (HOUST > 550k) reflects strong consumer demand for durable goods and services, increasing corporate revenues and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 40% of instances where HOUST exceeds 550k, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) and stable inflation (CPIAUCSL < 2.0%) create a favorable monetary environment, reducing borrowing costs and boosting equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Interaction term between FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL shows Rank IC < 0.03, or 60d returns are negative in > 40% of instances with both proxies indicating accommodative policy.

mechanism_narrative: During housing demand slumps (HOUST < 500k), rising retail sales (RSAFS > 350k) signal resilience in consumer spending, offsetting weak housing data and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Interaction term between HOUST and RSAFS shows Rank IC < 0.03, or 20d returns are negative in > 40% of instances where housing demand is weak but retail sales are strong.
